transcript.text: Oops.
Can you hear me?
I can.
You're very faint, though.
I don't know if that's me or me.
Oh, wait, no.
No, it's better, I guess.
No, you're better.
No, you're good.
I think my computer was just waking up.
Nice.
Hi, Mansi.
Hello.
Hi.
How are you doing?
Doing good.
How are you?
Good.
Hopefully this time is a little better for you.
I know it was very late last week.
Well, it's much better.
It's not little.
It's completely much better.
I know.
It's hard having evening meetings after a long day.
Do you know if it's just us today or if others will be joining?
No, I am expecting Rangtip to join the call.
Let me just check check with him.
Okay.
Just one.
And I'll check with Mahindra also.
I wanted him over the call.
Sounds good.
Hey.
Hi.
How's it going?
I'm good.
How are you?
Good.
Thanks.
It sounded like we might have one more person joining.
So I can just give them another minute if it works.
Mahindra wouldn't join.
So we can start.
Okay.
Great.
So it's nice to meet you, Ankit.
I'm Bailey.
I am going to be the new engagement manager joining these calls from now on.
So I'm going to share an agenda for today.
Um.
I I'm going share camera.
um

So I think today I wanted to introduce myself and also now that we had, I got to meet Mansi last week, but I wanted to just sort of before we kind of jump in, just to get a sense from you, from your team, kind of if you wanted to share anything about what's been going well so far or any areas we can improve or just sort of like general things you would would like me to focus on just to make sure in terms of your strategy or in terms of execution, just to make sure as we sort of hand off your account as I'm coming on that I don't miss anything, that nothing kind of falls between the cracks, things that you might have talked to Katya about.
All right.
Sounds good.
Nice to meet you as well.
And I'll do a bit of a round of an introduction for myself.
I lead everything SEO here.
I work closely with the web team.
And the brand team.
So we are all placed under the brand team.
It's been nice working with GrowthX.
I think it's been over two months now, if I'm not wrong, or even more.
I think Q3 was when we started.
And we started with discussing the vulnerability database as a project while it was in the ideation phase.
It got live the last month.
We are all prepping up for the phase two, which is nice to see.
And I wanted to understand, so you'd act as the account manager, or you'd also help on the content side?
So the way it's organized at GrowthX is we have these sort of engagement managers who essentially act as account managers.
So I'm the one who will be meeting with you weekly.
I'm the one who, you know, keeps track of your goals, make sure your strategy's working.
I will report.
Support on, like, content performance monthly and then also just on a week-to-week kind of keep track of how things are going.
So I'm sort of the owner of understanding your strategy, understanding your account, sort of setting your topic clusters.
I sort of support with keyword research.
And someone else, one of our managing editors or content managers actually runs the content and creates the content and is sort of owning the pipeline that creates that content and they edit it.
So we work very closely together because, obviously, how that content is written, it's important that it, you know, matches your goals.
But I don't actually run the content.

I'm sort of more owning your strategy and understanding when we need to kind of pull in expert-level support from our managing directors because we have a whole team of managing directors with different, very specific areas of expertise, like technical SEO, you know, more technical keyword research.
So I'm sort of...
The hub who kind of pulls in that assistance we need, who makes sure that the content is kind of up to your standards and is reaching our goals.
But I don't actually create it myself.
All right.
That's helpful.
Yeah.
And if there are things in terms of content quality, like if you have concerns or things that you really are pleased with, like I'm sort of your point of contact for all that.
And I make sure that those updates get made.
So I can still be that person you should talk to about the content quality.
So just so you know.
All right.
Sounds good.
Okay.
Well, I'm glad to hear things have been going well.
I had a couple kind of just more ops questions for you.
I tend to do sort of a monthly deep dive into reporting with clients.
And if anything changes or like there's anything to be concerned about on a week-to-week basis, I'll flag it.
And, but I find that often, like we.
Weekly reporting, like, it's just not enough time to see meaningful trends.
And I wasn't sure how you were treating reporting before and if that was, if this cadence kind of made sense for your goals and if it worked for you.
Yeah, I think monthly reporting is fine, but we'd continue to meet weekly, right?
Yes.
So it's up to you.
I think that is, some clients I meet with weekly, some we do every other week with an asynchronous update weekly.
So I think it just depends a little bit.
Sometimes when we're in a steady state and we feel like we have a long runway of content and when we meet, it's really just, there's not much to talk about.
Sometimes we'll go to a biweekly meeting, but I think for now, I feel like there's a lot going on.
I think weekly makes sense for now, but also I'm happy to, you know, change that depending on your preferences as well.
I think weekly is fine because we are on the leg for phase two for CVE database and there's supposedly...
weekly is fine.
I think weekly fine.
Some publishing that's going to happen week on week, at least for the next five, six weeks.
So for January, let's keep week on week, and then we can decide later.
Yeah, I agree.
And I just wanted to flag for you, Katia may have mentioned this already, but we do have a quiet week, so we don't have any meetings or content production, the week between Christmas and New Year's.
So that week, I will be canceling our meeting, but I'll send an async update at the beginning of that week, just so you can sort of see what's coming down the line.
But I just wanted to flag that for you.
And if your team, I know different teams have different holiday closures for their office, so I was wondering if there was any for you to sort of, that we should be aware of.
That's right.
So I was just checking my calendars on this side.
So I think we have one weekly on 24th of December and then 31st of December.
Let's.
I'll both of those because 24th of December is essentially a holiday for all of us here.
Okay.
So I'll remove those and not send like a longer update that covers that sort of that period before that as well.
Okay.
So since Marcus is here, maybe I'll let you give an update.
Oh, before actually we do, is the code freeze, has that been lifted?
I wanted to check in with you about that.
Yeah.
So it would be lifted today in the PSD time zone.
So probably by tomorrow when all of us get to work, I'll have an update.
Okay, great.
That sounds good.
All right.
Thank you.
And then Marcus, maybe I'll let you give an update on how the work is going here.
Actually, we are just waiting for the code freeze so we can start publishing more CVs.
So I'm working on our site to get the new list off.
Updated CVS to publish on this next round.
So that's the first item.
And as soon as the code freeze is left, we can start running the workflows and prepare new entries to publish.
Sounds good.
Yeah, I mean, I'd expect the same update.
So just a quick one.
I think we discussed this earlier.
Were you able to put together a diagram or something on what's really happening or just in terms of document?
Okay, I'll do that.
So, yeah, I missed that.
No worries.
I think if you get time for that, we could just prepare a quick one, a more actionable document so that we could have something in records of what's really happening.
Okay.
I can do it.
And then, Hankit, do you want to just...
And a Slack message kind of confirming when that code freeze is lifted?
Yep, sure.
Okay, great.
Okay.
Then in terms of the CS101 content, so the articles, I know, I think Mansi went in and left some comments on the table of contents for last week's articles.
So those are all being updated, and we will deliver those this week.
We also have five new articles that we're running now.
Something that I wanted to touch base about is, and I think Katia mentioned it last week, but I wasn't sure if Mansi had a chance to talk to your team about it.
Because the articles are quite long, I think once we start publishing the CVE content again, we sort of propose moving to maybe more like three to four editorial articles a week.
So,
Just to make sure we're able to kind of keep that same good quality, I think, because there are 3,000 words or more sometimes.
And usually we sort of aim for articles to be 1,500 to 2,000 words.
But I wanted to touch base with your team and see what you thought about that.
Can we keep at four per week?
I think we can manage four per week.
Okay.
Should be fine.
And then I did want to talk a little bit about strategy, sort of planning for January and beyond.
Taking a look at sort of the keywords that we have, you know, I think it seems like your team has been very focused on sort of these higher volume keywords.
And I'm wondering, you know, thinking just ahead in terms of your strategy.
You know, ahead in

What your thoughts were about kind of focusing on sort of longer tail, higher intent keywords, if that was part of your SEO strategy, if that was something you had sort of talked about and kind of, you know, rejected, or if that was something you were interested in pursuing.
I think we can explore.
For most of the products and solution areas, the SentinelOne essentially solves for.
We've tried to cover, and we've tried to cover where there is demand for.
So that's been largely the strategy, where we cover where there are searches for, we have content built for it.
And the idea was to get top in the funnel traffic and then leads following.
So that has been the strategy to plainly put.
But yes, we are open to new ideas.
Okay.

That might be something to think about for just, you know, maybe we revisit it in January because I think there could be, you know, we've seen good opportunity, especially I think for clients that are interested in AEO and appearing in, you know, LLM visibility.
Some of those longer tail, high intent keywords have seen a lot of getting a lot of traction, even on keywords with sort of lower volume, but sort of move further down the funnel.
So it's just something to keep in mind.

And then I also wanted to let you know that there are some new topics, article topics to review in the Airtable, sort of the goal, given the holidays and people being out of office, I'm hoping that we'll get, I think we have two weeks of approved content now, but it would be nice to sort of.
line up our editorial calendar so that our writers kind of knew what they were working on, I think, through early January, just to make sure we maintain our velocity.
So when you have a chance, it'd be great to go in and take a look at those.
I think it's only about 15 new topics, but we can add more as well if those don't feel like they're a good fit.
All right.
So we should have approvals or at least our comments.
Yeah, Mahindra started working on them.
Okay, great.
And then I wanted to touch base and see if there was anything.
I know Katya tended to touch base with you to see if there was anything that had been published on a weekly basis.

And I was wondering, just so I totally understand how you all have been kind of going in and using Airtable, is the way that you've been doing it, have you been updating content in there once it's been published?
And, Nancy, this may be...
A question for you, or do you tend to let the GrowthX team know on Slack that things have been published, and then we go in and update it?
So ideally, it was I would let Katia know about the articles that are being published.
But if you want, I can update on Airtable also.
I think I have the access for it.
You know, if you let us know on Slack, that's fine, too.
I just wanted to check and see sort of how you'd been using it to make sure that we didn't miss anything.
So I think as long as you let us know on Slack, if that's easiest for you, you can just let us know there, and then we can go in and update.
And then, Mansi, one last thing I wanted to touch base is I think you had mentioned when Katia was on the call last week that you had sort of.
About the project base.
So I did look back and I asked Yael, who is managing Prompt.
Engineering and Joe about the write-ups.
I still have to hear back from them, but I think hopefully by next week, we can have the revised talk.
Okay, great.
Thank you for following up on that.
Okay, I think that was everything that I had on my list that I wanted to cover.
Is there anything that your team wanted to talk about?
All good?
Okay.
Well, then, yeah, I'll deliver in Slack.
I think by Thursday, by tomorrow, we should be done with the five new articles for this week.
And then I think definitely, I think today or tomorrow, we'll also be done making the updates from last week's content.
So I will deliver those in Slack and let me know if there is anything else that comes up.
All right, sounds good.
All right, thanks so much, team.
Bye.
Thanks a lot.
Cheers.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce new GrowthX manager and align on project status and strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=101.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Manager:** Bailey Seybolt is the new GrowthX Engagement Manager, owning strategy and reporting while coordinating content production.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=215.0)
  - [**CVE Project Unblocked:** The code freeze lifts today (Dec 10, PST), unblocking Phase 2 CVE publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
  - [**Content Cadence Set:** Editorial articles will shift to 4/week to maintain quality on long-form content (\>3,000 words) while also publishing CVEs.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=636.0)
  - [**Strategy Shift Proposed:** GrowthX proposed exploring high-intent, long-tail keywords to capture lower-funnel traffic, a shift from the current top-of-funnel strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)

## Topics

### New Engagement Manager

  - [Bailey Seybolt is the new GrowthX Engagement Manager, replacing Katia.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=101.0)
  - [**Role:** Owns strategy, goals, and reporting. Coordinates content execution and pulls in expert support (e.g., technical SEO).](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=215.0)

### CVE Database Project

  - [**Blocker:** Phase 2 publishing is on hold pending the code freeze lift.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0)
  - [**Status:** The freeze lifts today (Dec 10, PST).](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
  - [**Action:** Ankit will confirm the lift in Slack, unblocking Marcus to prepare the next CVE list.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [**Documentation:** Marcus will create a process diagram for internal records.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)

### Content Strategy & Cadence

  - [**Cadence Adjustment:** Editorial articles will shift from 5/week to 4/week.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=636.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Maintain quality on long-form content (\>3,000 words) while also publishing CVEs.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=636.0)
  - [**Strategic Proposal:** GrowthX proposed exploring high-intent, long-tail keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Capture lower-funnel traffic and improve AEO/LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
      - [**Current Strategy:** Focus on high-volume keywords for top-of-funnel traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
      - [**Decision:** SentinelOne is open to exploring this further in January.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=713.0)

### Operations & Reporting

  - [**Reporting Cadence:** Monthly deep-dive reports are confirmed. Weekly meetings will continue through January due to the high volume of CVE publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
  - [**Holiday Schedule:**](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** No meetings or production Dec 25–Jan 1.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
      - [**SentinelOne:** Holidays on Dec 24 & 31.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
      - [**Decision:** All meetings on Dec 24 & 31 are canceled.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
  - [**Content Approvals:** \~15 new article topics are in Airtable for approval to ensure a smooth production pipeline through early January.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=817.0)
  - [**Workflow:** SentinelOne will continue notifying GrowthX via Slack when content is published, and GrowthX will update Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0)

### Prompt Engineering Project

  - [**Status:** Mansi is awaiting a revised scope from Yael and Joe.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [**ETA:** Next week.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Ankit:** Confirm code freeze lift in Slack (Dec 11).](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [**Marcus:** Create CVE publishing process diagram.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)
  - [**SentinelOne Team:** Review and approve new article topics in Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=817.0)
  - [**Mansi:** Provide Prompt Engineering project update (next week).](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [**Bailey:** Deliver 5 new articles and 5 revised articles via Slack (Dec 11).](https://fathom.video/share/mFFD6H-MX5sEz5CimijANyPjh1KqUcNv?tab=summary&timestamp=992.0)

action_items.description: Cancel Dec 24 & Dec 31 weekly syncs w/ SentinelOne; send async update week of Dec 22
Draft CVE Phase 2 workflow diagram/doc for Ankit
Slack Bailey when code freeze lifts; then GrowthX starts CVE publishing
Review 15 new topics in Airtable; add comments/approvals
Slack Bailey when articles publish; then GrowthX updates Airtable
Slack Ankit/Mansi: deliver 5 new CS101 articles; then deliver last week's updates
